l3_raw:
Findings: Bilateral pleural effusions, left greater than right; bibasilar airspace disease and atelectasis; no pneumothorax or acute cardiopulmonary abnormality. No evidence of active tuberculosis. Osseous structures unremarkable.

Impression: Moderate bilateral pleural effusion with associated basilar opacities consistent with compressive alveolar edema versus pneumonia. Left-sided opacity may represent loculated fluid collection within an empyematous bulla rather than simple consolidation given its homogeneous appearance without air bronchograms. Recommend follow-up imaging to evaluate resolution if clinically appropriate.

l3_raw:
Findings: No acute cardiopulmonary abnormality identified. Degenerative changes of thoracic spine and degenerative bony abnormalities noted bilaterally at shoulders, elbows, hips, knees, ankles, wrists, hands, feet, neck, jaw, skull base, ribs, clavicles, scapulae, vertebrae, sternum, pelvis, sacrum, coccyx, iliac bones, femoral heads/neck/thighs/tibia/fibulas/metatarsals/phalanges/malleoli/calcaneus/bones throughout body. Calcified granulomas within left upper lobe lung field consistent with prior infection or inflammation. Left shoulder joint shows post-traumatic deformities without evidence of active disease. Right breast implant is well-positioned. Cardiomediastinal silhouette unremarkable. Thoracolumbar spinal fusion hardware present. Biliary stent projecting over right hepatic region not clearly visualized but likely patent given lack of distension. Abdomen free of air under diaphragm except normal bowel gas pattern seen below stomach bubble level.

Unusual calcifications compatible with chronic inflammatory process (granulomatous) rather than malignancy. Chronic liver pathology possible due to presence of metallic density projected above dome of gallbladder which may represent retained surgical clips from cholecystectomy performed several years ago.

Findings: Cardiomegaly, no focal consolidation or pneumothorax. No pleural effusion. Degenerative changes of thoracic spine and calcified mediastinal lymph nodes noted.

Impression: Moderate cardiomegaly without acute pulmonary process. Calcification within right paratracheal region likely represents chronic granulomatous disease (e.g., old tuberculosis). Thoracostomy tube has been removed from left hemithorax since prior imaging study dated October 20th at our institution; however, there is persistent opacity overlying the posterior aspect of the heart which may represent residual fluid collection post-procedure rather than active pathology given lack of interval change compared to previous studies. Recommend follow-up with CT scan if clinically indicated by patient’s symptoms.

Impression: Mildly enlarged heart without evidence of congestive cardiac failure. Tortuosity and mild dilation of descending thoracic aorta noted. Left basilar opacity may represent scarring from prior pneumonia but is not definitively diagnostic as an active process given lack of interval change compared to previous films.

l3_raw:
Findings: No acute cardiopulmonary abnormality identified. Metallic density overlying left heart border consistent with prior surgical clips or foreign body, unchanged from previous imaging. Left apical lucency without lung markings is compatible with postoperative changes and/or pneumothorax; no active air leak seen at time of examination. Cardiomediastinal silhouette within normal limits. Bony structures intact.

Impression: Post-surgical metallic densities noted as stable compared to recent studies. Unchanged appearance since last study. Possible small residual pleural effusion versus early subpulmonic fluid level cannot be definitively excluded by plain film alone but unlikely given lack of associated findings such as elevated hemidiaphragm contour. Small right-sided hydropneumothorax not clearly demonstrated despite multiple views due to patient positioning during exam. Recommend follow-up CT scan if clinically indicated.

Mildly enlarged heart size, unchanged from previous examination. Left base opacity likely represents atelectasis or scarring; no focal consolidation to suggest pneumonia. Small left pleural effusion is unlikely given lack of homogeneous density with ill-defined borders seen elsewhere within lung fields. Right costophrenic angle blunting may represent chronic disease rather than active process. Thoracic spine degenerative spondylosis noted bilaterally without evidence of fracture.

Impression: Unremarkable post-CABG study. Possible mild pulmonary edema versus early congestive failure should be considered clinically if symptoms persist despite current therapy. Atelectatic change overlying left lower lobe suggests possible aspiration event requiring attention by physician as appropriate. Degenerative thoracolumbar osteoarthritis.

Right costophrenic angle blunting may represent chronic disease rather than active process. Thoracic spine degenerative spondylosis noted bilaterally without evidence of fracture.
l3_impression: Unremarkable post-CABG study. Possible mild pulmonary edema versus early congestive failure should be considered clinically if symptoms persist despite current therapy. Atelectatic change overlying left lower lobe suggests possible aspiration event requiring attention by physician as appropriate. Degenerative thoracolumbar osteoarthritis.
